cards.title: Keyman Insurance
cards.content:
ΓÇó Taken by a business on the life of its most crucial employee (whose death would cause huge financial loss).
ΓÇó The business pays the premium and is the beneficiary.
ΓÇó Premium is treated as a tax-deductible business expense.

cards.title: MWP Act (Sec 6)
cards.content:
ΓÇó Married Women's Property Act: A policy taken by a husband for his wife/children becomes a Trust.
ΓÇó The policy money CANNOT be touched by the husband's creditors, court attachments, or even the husband himself.

items.text: Mortgage Redemption: A decreasing term policy that pays off your home loan if you die.
Partnership Insurance: Ensures surviving partners have cash to buy out the deceased partner's share.
Estate Planning: Using life insurance to create immediate cash to pay estate taxes or debts.

sections.title: ≡ƒÆí Exam Master Tips
items: Keyman Insurance is strictly a TERM insurance policy. It does not have a savings component.
Under the MWP Act, once the policy is created, the insured loses all control over it.
Trustees: Must be appointed to manage the MWP policy on behalf of the wife/children.
Mortgage Redemption is the best way to secure a large debt like a Home Loan.
Credit Life Insurance protects the lender (bank) if the borrower dies before repaying the loan.
